SUBAM PAPERS: TO PROVIDE ₹40 CRORE CORPORATE GUARANTEE FOR WHOLLY-OWNED SUBSIDIARY 🏦📄

• 🛡️ Subam Papers has proposed to provide a ₹40 crore corporate guarantee in favour of YES Bank for credit facilities extended to its wholly owned subsidiary, Subam Paper and Boards Pvt. Ltd. 

• 💰 The guarantee will secure a ₹40 crore Cash Credit (CC) limit, comprising an existing ₹20 crore limit and a fresh ₹20 crore enhancement.  

• 🤝 The company clarified that promoters have no interest in the transaction, and the corporate guarantee has been provided on an arm's length basis. 

• ⚠️ The company stated there is no immediate financial impact, except that the guarantee will remain a contingent liability if the subsidiary fails to meet its repayment obligations. 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (The guarantee supports the subsidiary's financing needs but creates a contingent liability for the parent company without any immediate financial impact.)

GODREJ CONSUMER: INVESTS ₹200 CRORE IN GODREJ PET CARE VIA RIGHTS ISSUE 🐶💰

• 🏢 Godrej Consumer Products has invested ₹200 crore in its wholly owned subsidiary, Godrej Pet Care Ltd., by subscribing to a rights issue to fund the subsidiary's business operations, growth plans and capital requirements. 

• 🐾 The investment aims to strengthen GCPL's presence in the fast-growing pet care category, which the company has identified as a strategic growth area. 

• 💰 GCPL subscribed to 1.61 crore equity shares at a total consideration of ₹200 crore, representing a primary capital infusion into Godrej Pet Care. 

• 📊 Following the investment, GCPL continues to hold 100% ownership in Godrej Pet Care. The subsidiary generated ₹2.22 crore revenue (₹222.1 lakh) in FY26.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The investment reinforces GCPL's commitment to the high-growth pet care segment and supports the expansion of its new business vertical.)

• 🏆 Saatvik Green Energy's material subsidiary, Saatvik Solar Industries Pvt. Ltd., has received and accepted a ₹138 crore order from a renowned Independent Power Producer (IPP)/EPC player for the supply of Solar PV Modules. 

• ☀️ The order is for the domestic market and involves the commercial supply of Solar PV Modules. 

• ⏳ The contract is scheduled to be executed by December 2026, providing revenue visibility over the coming months. 

• 🤝 The company confirmed that the order is not a related-party transaction, and the promoter/promoter group has no interest in the customer awarding the contract. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The ₹138 crore order strengthens Saatvik's order book, enhances revenue visibility and reflects continued demand for its solar PV modules.)

reports.text:
HCLTECH: PARTNERS WITH TIM BRASIL TO ENABLE SOUTH AMERICA'S FIRST CROSS-PLATFORM eSIM TRANSFER 📱🌎

• 🤝 HCLTech has partnered with TIM Brasil to enable South America's first cross-platform eSIM transfer capability, enhancing the mobile experience for millions of users in Brazil.  

• 📲 Powered by HCLTech's Device Entitlement Gateway (DEG), the solution allows customers to securely transfer eSIM profiles across devices and operating systems without visiting stores or replacing physical SIM cards. 

• 🚀 The solution simplifies device upgrades, accelerates next-generation mobile service adoption, and delivers a seamless, secure and device-agnostic user experience. 

• 🤖 HCLTech said the collaboration leverages its AI-driven telecom platforms to help TIM Brasil build a more agile, AI-powered telecom ecosystem. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The partnership strengthens HCLTech's telecom digital transformation portfolio and reinforces its capabilities in AI-led network and customer experience solutions.)

reports.text:
VENTURA GUARANTY: NCLT APPROVES AMALGAMATION OF STEP-DOWN SUBSIDIARY WITH VENTURA SECURITIES ⚖️🔄

• 🏛️ The NCLT, Mumbai Bench has sanctioned the Scheme of Amalgamation of Ventura Allied Services Pvt. Ltd. (step-down subsidiary) with Ventura Securities Ltd. (subsidiary). The order was pronounced on July 21, 2026. 

• 🤝 The merger aims to simplify the group structure, enhance financial strength and flexibility, reduce administrative costs and improve the long-term competitiveness of the combined entity. 

• 📈 Since Ventura Allied Services is a wholly owned subsidiary of Ventura Securities, no consideration or share exchange will be paid. The existing investment will stand cancelled upon the merger. 

• 📋 The scheme received the required approvals from shareholders, creditors and regulators, including SEBI and stock exchanges, before receiving the NCLT's final sanction.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The internal restructuring simplifies the corporate structure, improves operational efficiency and strengthens the group's long-term governance and financial flexibility.)

PRAVEG: APPROVES ₹53.72 CRORE PREFERENTIAL ISSUE TO STRENGTHEN BALANCE SHEET 💰📈

• 🏢 Praveg's Board has approved a preferential issue aggregating ₹53.72 crore, comprising 8,33,700 equity shares and 11,00,000 convertible warrants, subject to shareholders' and regulatory approvals.  

• 💰 The company will issue 8,33,700 equity shares at ₹275 per share to convert an unsecured inter-corporate loan from Jhaveri Credits & Capital Ltd. into equity, reducing outstanding debt by approximately ₹22.93 crore. 

• 📑 Praveg will also issue 11,00,000 convertible warrants at ₹275 each to members of the Promoter & Promoter Group, aggregating approximately ₹30.25 crore. Each warrant will be convertible into one equity share. 

• 🚀 The company said the fund raise will strengthen its capital structure, improve financial flexibility, reduce debt and support long-term expansion in its eco-luxury hospitality business. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The preferential issue improves Praveg's balance sheet through debt reduction while providing fresh growth capital for future expansion.)

• 🌍 Through this partnership, JOJO TV will be available to TCL Smart TV users across India and the Middle East & North Africa (MENA), significantly expanding the platform's distribution footprint. 

• 🚀 The collaboration strengthens JOJO's presence across Connected TV (CTV) platforms, enhancing content accessibility through premium smart TV ecosystems. 

• 📈 The company said the partnership reinforces its multi-platform distribution strategy and supports its goal of reaching audiences across key domestic and international markets. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The partnership expands JOJO TV's reach on premium smart TV platforms, improving distribution, audience access and long-term digital growth prospects.)

SONA COMSTAR: FORMS TWO JVs WITH JAPAN'S DENSO FOR EV & HYBRID POWERTRAIN SYSTEMS 🤝⚡

• 🇯🇵 Sona Comstar has signed definitive agreements with DENSO Corporation, Japan to establish two joint ventures in India for the development, design, manufacturing and marketing of electric and hybrid powertrain systems across multiple vehicle segments. 

• 🚗 The first JV will develop high-voltage liquid-cooled traction inverters, traction motors and generators for electric and hybrid passenger and commercial vehicles. DENSO will hold 51% and Sona Comstar 49%. 

• 🛵 The second JV will focus on air-cooled traction inverters, motors, generators and e-Axles for two- and three-wheelers. DENSO will acquire a 49% stake in Sona Comstar's existing EV traction motor and controller business at an enterprise value of ₹1,750 crore, while Sona Comstar will retain 51% and management control. 

• 🚀 The partnership combines DENSO's global electrification technology with Sona Comstar's manufacturing and engineering expertise to accelerate innovation, expand product offerings and strengthen India's EV ecosystem. The transaction is subject to regulatory approvals.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The strategic partnership with DENSO significantly strengthens Sona Comstar's EV technology capabilities, expands its product portfolio and enhances long-term growth opportunities in electric and hybrid mobility.)

Apollo Micro Systems | Indian Navy Make-II Prototype Sanction

Project Highlights
- Received Make-II Prototype Sanction.
- Project for SAVIOR-ASW platform.
- Indigenous semi-submersible autonomous vessel.
- Designed for anti-submarine warfare surveillance.

Strategic Significance
- Entry into autonomous maritime warfare.
- Strengthens indigenous defence capabilities.
- Expands beyond defence electronics.
- Progressing towards complete defence systems.
- Enhances long-term technology portfolio.

Commercial Perspective
- Prototype approval, not production order.
- No immediate revenue impact.
- Successful development may enable future procurement.
- Commercial orders remain subject to Navy approval.

Key Monitorables
- Prototype development progress.
- Indian Navy validation process.
- Conversion into production contracts.
- Execution under Make-II framework.

Impact
- Positive.
- Strategic capability enhancement.
- Strengthens long-term defence positioning.
- Creates future order optionality.

Key Takeaway
- Apollo Micro Systems' Make-II Prototype Sanction for the SAVIOR-ASW project is strategically significant despite having no immediate revenue impact. It marks the company's entry into autonomous maritime defence and reflects its evolution from a defence electronics supplier to a developer of advanced indigenous defence systems. Successful execution and Navy validation could pave the way for future production opportunities.

reports.text:
HARISH TEXTILE: NCLT RESERVES ORDER IN INSOLVENCY PETITION ⚖️🏛️

• ⚠️ The NCLT, Mumbai Bench has reserved its order in the insolvency petition filed by M/s. Kamlesh Corporation against Harish Textile Engineers under Section 9 of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC), 2016.  

• 🏛️ The Tribunal declined any further adjournment, heard the applicant's submissions and confirmed that the company's reply will be considered before passing the final order.  

• 📅 The company became aware of the NCLT order on July 21, 2026, after it was uploaded on the NCLT website.  

• 📊 Harish Textile stated that there is no material impact on its financials or operations at this stage, and any financial impact will depend on the final outcome of the proceedings. 

🔴 Impact: Negative (The insolvency petition remains pending before the NCLT, creating legal uncertainty until the Tribunal delivers its final order.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
DEEPAK BUILDERS: COMPLETES JALANDHAR CANTT RAILWAY STATION REDEVELOPMENT 🚉🏗️

• 🏗️ Deepak Builders has successfully completed the redevelopment and construction of Jalandhar Cantt. Railway Station for Indian Railways, with the project now ready for inauguration. 

• 🚉 The redeveloped station features modern passenger amenities, upgraded platforms, improved circulation facilities, enhanced accessibility, contemporary architecture and advanced public utility infrastructure, transforming it into a modern transportation hub. 

• 📈 The company said the successful completion strengthens its credentials in the railway and transportation infrastructure sector, reinforces its execution capability for complex public infrastructure projects and enhances its competitiveness for future railway station redevelopment opportunities. 

• 🤝 Management believes the completion of this landmark project will enhance the company's reputation, stakeholder confidence and long-term business prospects. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Successful execution of a marquee Indian Railways project strengthens Deepak Builders' track record, improving its prospects for securing future infrastructure contracts.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
TEJASSVI AAHARAM: COMPLETES 100% ACQUISITION OF FUNK FOODS VIA ₹51.16 CRORE SHARE SWAP 🍽️🤝

• 🏢 Tejassvi Aaharam has completed the acquisition of 100% equity stake in Funk Foods Pvt. Ltd. (FFPL) by allotting 5.12 crore equity shares on a preferential basis through a share swap, with no cash outflow.  

• 💰 The acquisition is valued at ₹51.16 crore, with shares issued at ₹10 per share (at par) in exchange for the entire equity of FFPL. 

• 🍜 Funk Foods is engaged in the development, manufacturing and export of freeze-dried, clean-label food products, including instant chutneys, soup mixes, ready-to-eat meals, spreads, dips and sauces. It reported FY25 turnover of ₹7.97 crore.  

• 📈 The company said the acquisition will strengthen its existing food business, create synergies and benefit stakeholders. The transaction is not a related-party transaction. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition expands Tejassvi Aaharam's product portfolio through a share-swap transaction without cash outflow, supporting long-term growth and business integration.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
SEJAL GLASS: SECURES ₹121 CRORE ARCHITECTURAL GLASS ORDERS IN UAE & INDIA 🏗️🌍

• 🏆 Sejal Glass' UAE subsidiary has secured five architectural glass orders worth AED 27.6 million (≈₹71 crore) for prestigious hospitality, residential and healthcare projects across the UAE, strengthening its presence in the GCC region. 

• 🇮🇳 The company has also bagged architectural glass projects worth approximately ₹50 crore in India, covering commercial, residential and institutional projects. 

• 🪟 The orders involve the supply of high-performance architectural glass solutions, including insulated glass units (IGUs), solar-control glazing and laminated safety glass, with execution scheduled during 2026–2027.  

• 📈 The company stated that these orders are in addition to its regular order inflows, reflecting healthy business momentum and reinforcing its order pipeline. Customer names have not been disclosed due to competitive sensitivity.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The ₹121 crore order wins strengthen Sejal Glass' order book, improve revenue visibility and reinforce its presence in both domestic and international architectural glass markets.)

reports.text:
SUN TV: UK SUBSIDIARY ISSUES BONUS SHARES TO PARENT COMPANY 🇬🇧📺

• 🏢 The Board of SunRisers Leeds Limited (SRL), a wholly owned subsidiary of Sun TV Network (formerly Northern Superchargers Limited), has approved the issue of 99,999 bonus equity shares of GBP 1 face value each to Sun TV Network. 

• 📈 Following the bonus issue, Sun TV Network's holding in SRL will increase to 1,00,000 equity shares of GBP 1 each. 

• 🔄 The transaction is an internal capital restructuring and there will be no change in Sun TV Network's ownership percentage, as SRL will continue to remain a wholly owned subsidiary. 

• 🏏 SunRisers Leeds is the franchise previously known as Northern Superchargers Limited, reflecting the company's branding transition. 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (The bonus issue is an internal restructuring within a wholly owned subsidiary and does not change Sun TV Network's ownership or have any material financial impact.)

reports.text:
TVS MOTOR: LAUNCHES TVS RAIDER IN EGYPT, EXPANDS NORTH AFRICA PRESENCE 🏍️🌍

• 🌍 TVS Motor has launched the TVS Raider in Egypt, marking a key step in strengthening its footprint in North Africa and expanding its premium commuter motorcycle portfolio in the region. 

• 🏍️ The TVS Raider is powered by a 124.76cc air & oil-cooled 3V engine producing 12.9 PS power and 11.5 Nm torque, featuring a 5-speed gearbox, full digital LCD cluster, LED headlamp, monoshock suspension and USB charger. 

• 🚀 TVS said Egypt is a strategic gateway to North Africa, and the launch is aimed at attracting young urban riders while accelerating the company's international growth strategy. 

• 🛍️ The motorcycle is available through select dealerships in Egypt and will be rolled out across the dealer network by July 2026 in Black and Yellow colour options. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The Egypt launch strengthens TVS Motor's international expansion strategy, enhances its presence in North Africa and supports long-term export growth.)

INNOVISION: BAGS ₹97.53 CRORE NHAI TOLL COLLECTION CONTRACT 🛣️💰

• 🏆 Innovision has received a Letter of Award (LoA) from the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) for user fee collection and facility maintenance at the Biratiya Kalan Fee Plaza on the Bar–Bilara–Jodhpur section of NH-112, Rajasthan. 

• 💰 The contract is valued at ₹97.53 crore and covers toll collection along with the upkeep and maintenance of adjacent toilet blocks, including consumable items.  

• ⏳ The 1-year contract was awarded on July 21, 2026 through a competitive e-tendering process.  

• 🤝 The order has been awarded by a domestic entity, with no promoter interest and no related-party transaction involved.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The ₹97.53 crore NHAI contract strengthens Innovision's order book, enhances revenue visibility and reinforces its position in the toll management and infrastructure services business.)

reports.text:
TCS: STUDY SHOWS PHYSICAL AI GOING MAINSTREAM IN MANUFACTURING 🤖🏭

• 📊 TCS' Physical AI Readiness Report 2026 found that 77% of manufacturers expect significant transformation in warehouse operations, followed by 75% in assembly and manufacturing, highlighting rapid adoption of Physical AI. 

• 💰 The study showed no surveyed manufacturer plans to reduce Physical AI investments, while 26% intend to increase spending, indicating strong long-term commitment to AI-led transformation. 

• 🤖 Around 68% of manufacturers are still in experimental or non-deployment stages, with legacy systems, data infrastructure and workforce skills identified as the biggest hurdles to large-scale adoption. 

• 🤝 TCS is accelerating enterprise adoption through its Physical AI Gemini Experience Center developed with Google Cloud, helping manufacturers deploy AI-powered robotics, automation and intelligent factory solutions.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The report reinforces TCS' leadership in AI-driven manufacturing solutions and supports long-term growth opportunities in industrial digital transformation.)

EBIX: RBI APPROVES EBIXCASH WORLD MONEY TO UNDERTAKE TRADE REMITTANCES 🌍💳

• 🏦 EbixCash World Money has received RBI approval to undertake trade remittances, becoming India's first non-bank institution to receive this approval under its perpetual Authorised Dealer Category-II (AD-II) licence. 

• 🌐 The approval expands the company's regulated cross-border payment capabilities, enabling it to offer trade remittance services under the FEMA framework, a segment traditionally served by banks. 

• 📈 The expanded licence positions EbixCash to benefit from growing international trade, increasing MSME exports and rising cross-border financial flows, while offering integrated technology-driven payment solutions. 

• 🚀 The approval strengthens Ebix's financial services ecosystem across foreign exchange, remittances, payments and fintech, enhancing its long-term strategy of building a diversified cross-border payments platform. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The RBI approval significantly expands EbixCash's regulated payments business, strengthens its competitive position in cross-border financial services and opens a new long-term growth opportunity.)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
AZAD ENGINEERING: DELIVERS FIRST INDIGENOUS EXPENDABLE TURBOJET ENGINE TO DRDO 🚀🇮🇳

• 🚀 Azad Engineering has delivered its first indigenous expendable turbojet engine to DRDO and the Ministry of Defence after successfully manufacturing and assembling the engine components. 

• 🛡️ The delivery marks a major milestone in India's indigenous propulsion capabilities, supporting the country's Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative in aerospace and defence. 

• ⚙️ The achievement highlights Azad Engineering's expertise in precision manufacturing, advanced engineering and complex aero-engine systems, reinforcing its role in India's strategic defence programmes. 

• 🇮🇳 The engine will be used for development trials, contributing to India's long-term technological advancement in indigenous aerospace propulsion systems. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The successful delivery strengthens Azad Engineering's credentials in high-value defence manufacturing, enhances its strategic positioning and opens opportunities in indigenous aerospace programmes.)

TRISHAKTI INDUSTRIES: ENTERS WIND ENERGY EQUIPMENT RENTAL SEGMENT 🌬️🏗️

• 🌬️ Trishakti Industries has announced its strategic entry into the wind energy equipment rental segment, expanding beyond its existing heavy equipment rental business to capitalize on India's fast-growing renewable energy market. 

• 🏗️ The company plans to develop capabilities in specialized 900-tonne heavy-lift cranes, which are increasingly required for installing next-generation, taller and higher-capacity wind turbines. 

• 🇮🇳 Management highlighted that India's installed wind power capacity is targeted to increase nearly 8x from ~13 GW to 100 GW between FY25 and FY30, creating a significant long-term opportunity for specialized equipment rental providers. 

• 🚀 The company will evaluate investments based on customer demand and deployment plans while continuing to expand its fleet and strengthen its position in specialized infrastructure equipment rentals. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The expansion into specialized wind energy equipment rental opens a new high-growth business vertical with strong long-term demand and high entry barriers.)

reports.text:
WANBURY: USFDA INSPECTION ENDS WITH 6 FORM 483 OBSERVATIONS 🏭🔍

• 🇺🇸 Wanbury has successfully completed a routine USFDA cGMP inspection at its Tanuku, Andhra Pradesh manufacturing facility, conducted between July 13 and July 17, 2026. 

• 📋 At the conclusion of the inspection, the USFDA issued a Form 483 with six observations, and the company will respond to the observations within the stipulated timeline. 

• 📝 Wanbury stated that its preliminary assessment indicates all six observations are addressable through supporting documentation, corrective actions, technical justifications and procedural enhancements. 

• 🌍 The company does not expect any impact on manufacturing operations, exports or financial performance arising from the inspection and remains committed to maintaining the highest cGMP standards. 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Receipt of Form 483 with six observations requires corrective action, but the company expects no material impact on operations or exports.)

• 🏗️ Talbros Engineering has secured a new industrial premises at Plot No. 38, Sector-4, Faridabad, Haryana, measuring 6,500 sq. ft., to support its business operations and future expansion.

reports.text:
ETERNAL: TRANSFERS 'NUGGET BY ZOMATO' BUSINESS TO WHOLLY-OWNED SUBSIDIARY 🤖🔄

• 🤝 Eternal (formerly Zomato) has approved the transfer of its 'Nugget by Zomato' business to its wholly owned subsidiary Carthero Technologies Pvt. Ltd. (CTPL) through a Business Transfer Agreement (BTA) as part of an internal restructuring. 

• 🤖 Nugget is a B2B AI-driven customer support platform, and CTPL will continue operating and expanding this business. 

• 💰 The business will be transferred for a cash consideration of ₹35 crore, with the transaction expected to be completed within 30 days of signing the agreement. 

• 📊 The Nugget business contributed ₹7.20 crore revenue (0.07%) and had a net worth of ₹10.54 crore (0.03%) in FY26. The company clarified that the transaction is a related-party transaction conducted at arm's length. 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (The transfer is an internal restructuring to streamline the corporate structure and does not materially impact Eternal's overall business or financials.)

• 💰 JD Cables reported Q1 FY27 revenue of ₹81.81 crore, registering a 68% YoY growth from ₹48.78 crore in Q1 FY26, driven by strong execution across its cable and conductor businesses. 

• 🏭 The company commenced commercial production at its new conductor manufacturing facility in Dankuni, West Bengal, enhancing production capacity and operational efficiency. 

• 🏦 JD Cables' Infomerics credit rating was upgraded from IVR BBB-/Stable to IVR BBB/Stable, reflecting its improving financial profile and enhanced creditworthiness. 

• 🌍 The company offers a diversified portfolio of power cables, conductors and transmission products, is an approved vendor for multiple State Electricity Boards (SEBs) and has an established presence across 15+ states.

reports.text:
BAJEL PROJECTS: FORMALISES ₹400+ CRORE EGYPT TRANSMISSION LINE AGREEMENT 🌍⚡

• 🤝 Bajel Projects has formalised its agreement with the Egyptian Electricity Transmission Company (EETC) for the construction of Lot 1 and Lot 5 of a 500 kV Overhead Transmission Line (OHTL) in Cairo, Egypt.  

• 💰 The combined order is valued at over ₹400 crore, forming part of Egypt's national grid reinforcement programme to strengthen its high-voltage transmission network. 

• 🌍 The signing ceremony was attended by India's Ambassador to Egypt, H.E. Suresh K. Reddy, highlighting the strategic importance of the India-Egypt infrastructure partnership. 

• 🚀 The agreement further strengthens Bajel's international EPC presence in the MENA region, building on its Saudi Arabia JV and Abu Dhabi subsidiary, while reinforcing its position in high-voltage transmission projects globally. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The formalisation of the ₹400+ crore international order strengthens Bajel Projects' overseas order book, enhances execution visibility and expands its global transmission EPC footprint.)

ORIENT ELECTRIC: STRONG Q1 WITH 24% REVENUE GROWTH, FANS & WIRES DRIVE PERFORMANCE ⚡📈

• 💰 Revenue rose 23.5% YoY to ₹950 crore, while PAT jumped 79.7% YoY to ₹31 crore. EBITDA increased 44.5% YoY to ₹67 crore, with EBITDA margin expanding to 7.0%.  

• 🌬️ Electrical Consumer Durables (ECD) revenue grew 22.7% YoY to ₹669 crore, led by market share gains in fans, ~36% growth in BLDC fans, double-digit growth in both DTM & MD markets, and strong export & e-commerce momentum. 

• 💡 Lighting & Switchgear revenue increased 25.4% YoY to ₹281 crore. Consumer lighting delivered double-digit growth, wires sales more than doubled, switchgear recorded double-digit growth, and premium lighting mix reached 60%. 

• 🚀 The company continued its innovation push with industry-first products such as Aero O2 (oxygen enriching fan), Aerosilent (India's most silent fan) and Ecotech Volt (battery backup fan), alongside several new launches across fans, lighting, wires and switchgear.  

• 📈 Management highlighted continued premiumisation, addition of ~3,600 retailers, high double-digit growth in e-commerce and exports, AI-enabled customer service initiatives, and ~₹10 crore cost savings from operational efficiency programs. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Broad-based growth across businesses, improving profitability, premiumisation and strong traction in fans, wires and lighting support a positive outlook.)

HFCL: APPROVES ₹215 CRORE AI DATA CENTRE CONNECTIVITY PRODUCTS FACILITY 🏭🚀

• 🏗️ HFCL's Board has approved setting up a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility for Data Center Connectivity Products with an annual capacity of 2.7 lakh assemblies at an estimated capital outlay of ₹215 crore. 

• 🤖 The investment is driven by the surging global demand for AI, hyperscale data centres, cloud computing, high-performance computing and high-speed networking, creating significant business opportunities in domestic and international markets. 

• 🔌 The facility will manufacture Miniature Multi-Fiber (MMC) and Super High-Density Multi-Fiber Termination (SNMT) assemblies used in high-speed data centres and AI infrastructure and is expected to be commissioned by September 2027.  

• 🌍 The project will be funded through an appropriate mix of internal accruals and debt and is expected to strengthen manufacturing capabilities, expand exports, support import substitution and reinforce HFCL's position in next-generation optical connectivity solutions. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The ₹215 crore expansion strengthens HFCL's presence in the fast-growing AI and data centre connectivity market, creating long-term growth opportunities.)

MAHINDRA LIFESPACES: EXPANDS CHENNAI INDUSTRIAL PARK JV WITH SUMITOMO 🏭🤝

• 🤝 Mahindra Lifespace's subsidiary Mahindra World City Developers (MWCDL), Sumitomo Corporation, Japan, and Mahindra Industrial Park Chennai (MIPCL) have signed a Second Supplemental Agreement to expand their existing industrial park partnership.  

• 🏗️ The agreement provides for the development of Phase 2B of the Chennai Industrial Park in Tamil Nadu, extending the existing joint venture under which Phase 1 and Phase 2A were developed. 

• 📊 Mahindra Lifespaces holds 89% stake in MWCDL, while MWCDL owns 60% of MIPCL and Sumitomo Corporation holds the remaining 40%. 

• 🌏 Under the agreement, MWCDL will continue to nominate the majority of directors on MIPCL's board, with the collaboration aimed at further expanding the industrial and manufacturing ecosystem in Chennai. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The expansion of the Chennai industrial park strengthens Mahindra Lifespaces' industrial real estate portfolio and supports long-term growth through continued collaboration with Sumitomo.)

Biopharma SHAKTI Scheme | Key Highlights & Potential Beneficiaries

Scheme Overview
- Outlay of ₹10,000 crore.
- Five-year implementation period.
- Strengthen India's biopharma ecosystem.
- Focus on biologics and biosimilars.
- Improve global competitiveness.
- Promote affordable healthcare.

Key Focus Areas
- Biopharma discovery funding.
- Equity support for innovation.
- National Biopharma R&D Network.
- NIPER biopharma ecosystem.
- Fermentation-based bulk drugs.
- Biologics manufacturing incentives.
- Drug delivery & packaging ecosystem.
- Biosimilars manufacturing support.

Clinical Research Push
- 1,000 accredited clinical trial sites.
- Led by ICMR.
- Faster clinical development.
- Strengthen CDSCO capabilities.
- Dedicated scientific reviewers.
- Better regulatory efficiency.

Expected Industry Impact
- Higher R&D investments.
- Stronger domestic manufacturing.
- Reduced import dependence.
- Faster biologics development.
- Boost to CRDMO industry.
- Increased global outsourcing.
- Enhanced innovation ecosystem.

Potential Beneficiaries
- Biocon – Biosimilars & biologics leader.
- Syngene International – CRDMO & biologics research.
- Laurus Labs – CDMO & biotechnology expansion.
- Divi's Laboratories – Custom synthesis & APIs.
- Aurobindo Pharma – Biosimilars & injectables.
- Dr. Reddy's Laboratories – Biosimilars & biologics pipeline.

Most Direct Beneficiaries
- Syngene International.
- Biocon.
- Laurus Labs.

Impact
- Strong Positive.
- Long-term structural policy support.
- Encourages innovation-led growth.
- Strengthens India's global biopharma position.

Key Takeaway
- The ₹10,000 crore Biopharma SHAKTI Scheme is a landmark policy aimed at building India's biologics and biosimilars ecosystem through funding, manufacturing incentives, clinical research infrastructure and regulatory reforms. Companies with strong capabilities in biologics, biosimilars and CRDMO services—particularly Biocon, Syngene International, Laurus Labs, Divi's Laboratories, Aurobindo Pharma and Dr. Reddy's Laboratories—are well positioned to benefit from this multi-year structural growth opportunity.

APOLLO MICRO SYSTEMS: WINS INDIAN NAVY MAKE-II PROTOTYPE SANCTION ORDER ⚓🇮🇳

• 🛡️ Apollo Micro Systems has been awarded a Make-II Prototype Sanction Order (PSO) by the Indian Navy for its indigenous Naval Maritime Electronic Warfare capabilities. 

• 🚤 The project involves the design, development and prototype demonstration of SAVIOR-ASW (Semi-Submersible Autonomous Vessel for Intelligence, Operations and Reconnaissance) under the Make-II category. 

• 🤖 SAVIOR-ASW is an unmanned semi-submersible platform for persistent Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW) surveillance, featuring AI/ML-based target classification, advanced acoustic sensors and encrypted multi-channel communication systems. 

• 🚀 The company said the order marks its formal entry into autonomous maritime and underwater warfare systems. Under the Make-II framework, the Government has no cost obligation during prototype development, with procurement envisaged upon successful prototype demonstration. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The prestigious Indian Navy Make-II order strengthens Apollo Micro Systems' defence portfolio, enhances its position in next-generation naval technologies and opens long-term procurement opportunities.)

BHARAT FORGE: SIGNS MoU WITH FLYING WHALES FOR HEAVY-LIFT AIRSHIPS ✈️🤝

• 🤝 Bharat Forge has signed a strategic MoU with French-Canadian aerospace company FLYING WHALES to jointly develop, manufacture and deploy heavy-lift airships for India's defence and strategic applications. 

• 🇮🇳 The partnership aims to establish Make in India production of next-generation airships, supporting the Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative by building an indigenous airship ecosystem for sovereign defence capabilities. 

• ✈️ The collaboration will focus on the LCA60T heavy-lift airship, capable of carrying up to 60 tonnes of cargo with vertical take-off and landing (VTOL) capability, hybrid-electric propulsion and minimal ground infrastructure requirements. 

• 🛡️ The airships are expected to support military logistics, transport of oversized equipment, ISR missions, communication relay, disaster relief and rapid deployment in remote border regions, while also creating high-technology jobs, an advanced supply chain and export opportunities. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The partnership strengthens Bharat Forge's aerospace and defence portfolio, creates a new indigenous manufacturing opportunity and enhances long-term growth in strategic defence technologies.)

Cyient DLM: Guidance - - Concall Update

• Management expects to maintain a book-to-bill ratio of 1.5x for the full year, following a robust 1.5x in Q1 FY '27, which contributed to the highest-ever order book of INR 2,598 crores.

• The company anticipates continued strong revenue momentum for FY '27, with similar trends expected in Q2 and beyond, positioning for sustained growth throughout the year.

• EBITDA margins are projected to expand from the current 9-11% in the "Strengthen" phase to 11-13% in the "Expand" phase (FY '27-'29) and further to 13-18% in the "Transform" phase (FY '30 and beyond).

• New segments like AI data centers and robotics are expected to see order intake this year, with revenue contributions primarily in FY '28 and FY '29, and the semiconductor capital equipment segment is anticipated to show revenue growth in the next 6 to 12 months.

• The company does not foresee needing additional CapEx beyond regular annual spending, as existing capabilities are largely sufficient for expansion into new segments.

PRIMO CHEMICALS: CAREEDGE WITHDRAWS BANK FACILITY CREDIT RATINGS 📄

• 🏦 CareEdge Ratings has withdrawn the outstanding credit ratings assigned to the bank facilities of Primo Chemicals, in line with its rating withdrawal policy. 

• 📝 The withdrawal was carried out at the company's request after receiving the required No Objection Certificates (NOCs) from the concerned lending banks. 

• 📅 CareEdge communicated the withdrawal through its letter dated July 21, 2026. 

• 📢 The company clarified that it had already disclosed the earlier credit ratings assigned to its bank loan facilities on June 26, 2026. 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (The withdrawal is an administrative action initiated by the company after obtaining lender NOCs and does not, by itself, indicate any change in Primo Chemicals' financial performance or credit quality.)

ZAPPFRESH: Q1 REVENUE JUMPS 58% YoY TO ₹74 CRORE, RETAIL NETWORK REACHES 200 OUTLETS 🛒📈

• 📈 Zappfresh reported provisional Q1 FY27 revenue of approximately ₹74 crore, up 58% YoY, while the combined business is operating at a pro-forma quarterly revenue run-rate of ₹85 crore following the Meevaa Foods acquisition.  

• 🏪 The company expanded its co-branded retail network to around 200 outlets, surpassing its FY27 target of 150 outlets ahead of schedule, with plans for further expansion. 

• 🛍️ Zappfresh added 70+ enterprise customers, expanded across Amazon, Blinkit and modern retail, commenced Blinkit deliveries in Mumbai, and continued scaling Meevaa Foods across retail and quick-commerce channels. 

• 🌍 The company strengthened its farm-to-fork ecosystem by onboarding 10 new sourcing partners, working with 1,700+ seafood farmers, expanding into UK, Canada and GCC markets, and reiterated its target of ₹600 crore revenue by FY28 with 18–20% EBITDA margins. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong revenue growth, successful Meevaa integration, rapid retail expansion and reaffirmed long-term guidance strengthen Zappfresh's growth outlook.)

MOTHERSON: COMPLETES ACQUISITION OF 81% STAKE IN JAPAN'S YUTAKA GIKEN 🇯🇵🤝

• 🤝 Samvardhana Motherson has completed the acquisition of an 81% stake in Yutaka Giken Co., Ltd. (YGCL) through its indirect wholly owned subsidiary Motherson Global Investments B.V. (MGI BV). 

• 🏭 As part of the transaction, Yutaka Giken completed the buyback of shares held by Honda Motor, resulting in 81% ownership by MGI BV and 19% by Honda Motor. 

• 📅 The final closing of the transaction was completed on July 21, 2026, following all required approvals and completion of conditions. 

• 🌍 Yutaka Giken is a Japan-based listed automotive component manufacturer, while its subsidiary Shinnichi Kogyo is also part of the acquired business, strengthening Motherson's global automotive portfolio. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition strengthens Motherson's global automotive components business, expands its presence in Japan and supports long-term growth through portfolio diversification.)

MANBA FINANCE: LAUNCHES EV BATTERY REPLACEMENT LOANS FOR E-3 WHEELERS 🔋🛺

• ⚡ Manba Finance has entered the EV battery financing segment by launching Battery Replacement Loans for electric three-wheelers (e-rickshaws and e-carts), expanding its product portfolio. 

• 💰 The new offering provides small-ticket, short-tenure loans to finance replacement lithium-ion batteries and charger kits, helping owner-operators replace batteries without disrupting their earnings. 

• 📲 The product features digital onboarding, e-mandate-based repayments and IoT-enabled batteries, with batteries sourced from quality-certified OEMs offering warranty and service support. 

• 🚀 The rollout will begin with Manba's existing electric three-wheeler customers before expanding to the broader market, deepening the company's presence in India's fast-growing EV ecosystem. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The new financing product broadens Manba's lending portfolio, strengthens customer retention and creates a new growth opportunity in the rapidly expanding EV ecosystem.)

• 🤝 Paras Defence's subsidiary Paras Semiconductors Pvt. Ltd. has signed an MoU with MP State Electronics Development Corporation (MPSeDC) to establish a greenfield advanced IC packaging OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) facility in Madhya Pradesh. 

• 💰 The proposed project involves an investment of approximately ₹6,200 crore and will be set up in the Indore–Ujjain region of Madhya Pradesh. 

• ⚙️ The facility will undertake advanced semiconductor packaging operations, including 3D heterogeneous integration, 2.5D/3D packaging, hybrid bonding, ultra-high-density fan-out packaging, chiplet integration, wafer bumping, flip-chip assembly, testing and reliability. 

• 🚀 The collaboration aims to combine the expertise of Paras Semiconductors and MPSeDC to strengthen India's semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem through advanced IC packaging capabilities. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The proposed ₹6,200 crore OSAT facility significantly strengthens Paras Defence's semiconductor ambitions, creating a long-term growth opportunity in India's strategic electronics manufacturing ecosystem.)

Polycab on Data Centre Opportunity in India (Q1FY27 Concall)

"We have read reports where the estimation is 8 to 16 GW or 18 GW from the current 1.8 GW. So we estimate that 1 megawatt translates into around INR 3.5 crores worth of cables, with 50% to 60% being conventional and the balance being optical fibre. So a sizable chunk of demand can come from the data centre. It's just a matter of timing. Definitely, an INR 20,000 to 25,000 crores market is there, but that's over a period of maybe 6-8 years. 

Through our distribution channel, we have supplied a majority of the conventional pieces in the existing installations. I think we have called out earlier publicly that in the data centres of Vodafone Idea in Mohali, Pune, and South India, we have supplied the cables."

reports.text:
TRUMP'S GENERIC DRUG TARIFF PLAN: WHAT IT MEANS FOR INDIAN PHARMA

• 💊 0% tariff on imported generic drugs from Aug 1, 2026 for the next 2 years, providing near-term relief to exporters

• 🏭 After the 2-year window, tariffs proposed to rise to 100% for one year, followed by 200% thereafter, encouraging companies to manufacture in the US

• ⏳ The two-year transition period gives Indian pharma companies time to reassess supply chains and accelerate US manufacturing investments

• 📈 Near-term beneficiaries: Aurobindo Pharma, Dr. Reddy's Laboratories and Lupin, as exports and pricing remain largely unaffected in the immediate future

• ⚠️ Long-term: Companies with significant US generic exposure may need to expand local manufacturing to avoid steep tariffs from 2028 onward

🟢 Near-term Impact: Positive for Indian generic exporters.

🟠 Long-term Impact: Mixed, depending on each company's US manufacturing footprint.

reports.text:
JPM on Bandhan BK
Neutral, TP Rs 175

Guidance cut was key focus in Bandhan’s 1QFY27 earnings call with a significant cut in FY27 RoA and growth targets.

Bandhan has cut 4QFY27 RoA guidance by 40bps to 1.2-1.4% (vs 1.6-1.8% earlier) driven primarily by external factors:
(i) 30bps RoA impact from moderation in NIM outlook (from 6.5% to 1Q level of 6.2%) on account of tighter liquidity and elevated funding costs, and
(ii) 10bps RoA impact from elevated opex especially rising technology related costs (from 4.2% to 1Q level of 4.3%).

Also, FY27 loan growth guidance was cut to lower end of the 14-15% range guided earlier with a conscious growth slowdown owing to rising AQ risk from higher energy costs and supply chain disruptions as well as uncertain monsoons.

It has done 1% RoA in Q1FY27 but has cut exit Q4FY27 RoA guidance to 1.2-1.4% (vs 1.6-1.8% earlier). The cut is primarily due to higher opex (tech spends, LOS etc) and stable NIM (vs improvement earlier).

*RoA guidance revised downwards*

- Management _*cut FY27 exit Q4FY27 RoA guidance from 1.6-1.8% to 1.2-1.4%*_ due to the challenging external environment like elevated funding cost, energy crisis due to West Asia war, unpredictable monsoons and higher tech costs.

- The ~40bps cut in guided RoA was attributed to NIM (which now looks stable) and ~10bps impact on opex. Opex is towards tech spends etc.

- Maintain growth guidance of 14% YoY for FY27

- *NIM outlook*: NIM of the bank was stable QoQ at 6.2% as against earlier expectation of NIM expansion, which the management attributed to elevated funding costs.

Tatva Chintan | Semiconductor Chemicals Opportunity

India's Semiconductor Push
- India investing ₹1.64 lakh crore in chip fabs.
- Supply chain remains largely import dependent.
- 85–90% semiconductor chemicals imported.
- Most equipment also sourced overseas.

Technical Challenge
- Semiconductor chemicals require ultra-high purity.
- Impurities measured in parts per billion.
- Tiny contamination can destroy entire chip batches.
- Supplier qualification is lengthy and stringent.

Tatva Chintan's Progress
- Developing five semiconductor-grade chemicals.
- One product cleared factory-level qualification tests.
- Commercial sales still around two years away.
- Three to four additional qualification rounds pending.

Commercialization Hurdles
- Chipmakers rarely switch qualified suppliers.
- MNC distributors validate and market products.
- Qualification process spans several years.
- Reliability matters more than pricing.

Industry Perspective
- Domestic fabs are being established.
- Supporting chemical ecosystem remains underdeveloped.
- Industry estimates suggest $80 billion support needed by 2035.
- Supply-chain localization will be gradual.

Key Takeaway
- Tatva Chintan has achieved an important technical milestone by qualifying its first semiconductor-grade chemical, but meaningful commercialization is still a multi-year process. India's semiconductor opportunity is significant, yet building a domestic chemicals ecosystem will require time, capital, and successful qualification across the entire supply chain.

reports.text:
GENERIC PHARMA TARIFF ANNOUNCEMENT BY TRUMP

1) All generic drugs being brought into the US will continue having ‌a tariff of 0% for two years starting from August 1. 

2) After 2 years the tariff rate will be raised to 100% for one year and to 200% thereafter.

Analysts Say-

1) Aurobindo has already build a substantial local manufacturing presence hence it can benefit from this, if implemented.

2) Dr. Reddy's, Lupin, Cipla and Zydus have some manufacturing footprint in the US, but that can not be called meaningful enough hence can get impacted hence this is sentimentally negative.

3) Alkem and Torrent are fully dependent on Indian manufacturing units, but they don't generate enough cashflows from US generics hence neutral impact.

4) Biocon is also depending on India and Malasian units for biosimilar and generic manufacturing and may see sentimentally negative impact.

5) Senores Pharma can benefit due to their local manufacturing presence for US generics market. 

In conclusion, this is difficult to implement policy as 90% of the US market by volumes is generic in nature. Aurobindo and Senores can benefit, if implemented and other large players may have to invest in the US and may see sentimentally negative impact.

PHARMA STOCKS ;  TRUMP ANNOUNCES PHASED TARIFF PLAN FOR GENERIC DRUG IMPORTS

• 💊 Generic drug imports to the US to continue at 0% tariff from Aug. 1, 2026 for 2 years

• ⏳ Tariff to rise to 100% for 1 year, followed by 200% thereafter

• 🏭 Aim is to encourage generic drug manufacturers to set up production in the US

• ⚕️ Policy does not apply to patented, branded or innovative drugs

🇮🇳 Likely Impact on Indian Pharma

🟢 Near-term (Positive):

No immediate tariff impact for the next two years, giving Indian exporters business continuity.

Companies with strong US generic exposure such as Sun Pharma, Dr. Reddy's, Aurobindo Pharma, Lupin, Cipla, Zydus Lifesciences, Glenmark get time to plan.

🔴 Long-term (Negative if implemented):

Exporters without US manufacturing could face significantly higher tariffs after the transition period.

Companies with existing or planned US manufacturing facilities would be relatively better positioned.

ANTONY WASTE: CORE BUSINESS GROWS, PCMC PLANT RECOVERY UNDERWAY ♻️

reports.text:
ACCIDENT TOMORROW: BANDHAN BANK ? 

GUIDANCE CUT 

It has done 1% RoA in Q1FY27 but has cut exit Q4FY27 RoA guidance to 1.2-1.4% (vs 1.6-1.8% earlier). The cut is primarily due to higher opex (tech spends, LOS etc) and stable NIM (vs improvement earlier).

*RoA guidance revised downwards*

- Management _*cut FY27 exit Q4FY27 RoA guidance from 1.6-1.8% to 1.2-1.4%*_ due to the challenging external environment like elevated funding cost, energy crisis due to West Asia war, unpredictable monsoons and higher tech costs.

- The ~40bps cut in guided RoA was attributed to NIM (which now looks stable) and ~10bps impact on opex. Opex is towards tech spends etc.

- Maintain growth guidance of 14% YoY for FY27

- *NIM outlook*: NIM of the bank was stable QoQ at 6.2% as against earlier expectation of NIM expansion, which the management attributed to elevated funding costs.

Atlanta Electricals Ltd Q1FY27  
 
➤ Revenue ₹466.33 Cr (+48.0% YoY)

➤ EBITDA ₹77.10 Cr (+58.1% YoY)
EBITDA Margin 16.5% (+105 bps YoY)

➤ PAT ₹46.84 Cr (+50.4% YoY)
PAT Margin 10.0% (+16 bps YoY)

➤ Revenue growth was driven by healthy execution, higher capacity utilization, and strong demand from the power transmission & distribution and renewable energy sectors

➤ Order Book ₹3,116.63 Cr as of June 30, 2026 (+25.0% QoQ), with Q1 order inflows of ₹792.42 Cr, providing strong revenue visibility

➤ Business mix continues to shift towards high-capacity transformers, with products rated 220 kV and above contributing a larger share of revenue

➤ Over 55% of the order book comprises 220 kV transformers, while 400 kV transformers and reactors contributed over ₹275 Cr in new orders

➤ Secured a ₹291.68 Cr order from Rajasthan Rajya Vidyut Prasaran Nigam Ltd. (RVPNL) for multiple high-capacity transformers

➤ Continued strengthening capabilities in EHV and UHV transformers, with approvals for 400 kV class transformers and ongoing investments to address opportunities in transmission, renewable energy, BESS, data centres, and exports

➤ Management Commentary:-
✓ Q1 FY27 started on a strong note with 48.0% YoY revenue growth, 58.1% YoY EBITDA growth, and 50.4% YoY PAT growth, driven by expanded manufacturing capacity, disciplined execution, and improved operating leverage

✓ Order book increased 25% QoQ to ₹3,116.63 Cr, providing strong revenue visibility and reflecting robust demand across transmission & distribution, renewable energy, and industrial applications

✓ Business mix is steadily shifting towards higher-capacity transformers, increasing the share of premium products in both the order book and revenue, supporting long-term earnings quality

✓ Management remains optimistic about long-term growth, supported by India's expanding power infrastructure, transmission network investments, renewable energy integration, battery energy storage systems (BESS), industrial electrification, and data centre demand

✓ Company continues to focus on higher capacity utilisation, expanding exports, commissioning its Inverter Duty Transformer facility, progressing backward integration, and strengthening capabilities in EHV/UHV transformers, including 400 kV and 765 kV products

VEDANTA OIL & GAS: ONGC MOVES DELHI HC TO ENFORCE $307 MN ARBITRAL AWARD ⚖️

• 🏛️ ONGC has filed an enforcement petition in the Delhi High Court seeking enforcement of an arbitral award dated July 31, 2023

• 💰 Arbitration award amounts to approximately US$307 million, applicable to Vedanta Oil & Gas and its subsidiaries

• 📒 Company said the amount has already been provided for in its books of account

• ⚖️ Delhi High Court has issued notice to the company to file its reply; no interim adverse order has been passed

• 📅 Next hearing is scheduled for September 11, 2026

• 📝 Vedanta Oil & Gas said it is evaluating the matter and will pursue appropriate legal remedies

JSW INFRA Q1 FY27: CARGO VOLUMES UP 6%, REVENUE RISES 18% ⚓📈

• 🚢 Cargo volumes increased 6% YoY to 31 million tonnes, led by Jaigarh Port and strong performance at Dharamtar, South West Port, Ennore Bulk Terminal & Tuticorin Terminal

• 💰 Consolidated operating revenue rose 18% YoY to ₹1,445 crore

JSW INFRA Q1 FY27: KEY BUSINESS UPDATES ⚓🚢

• ⚓ Expanded cargo handling capacity at South West Port (Goa) from 11 MTPA to 12 MTPA and Mangalore Container Terminal from 4.2 MTPA to 6.0 MTPA

• 🚆 Received environmental clearance and rail connectivity approval to the Dedicated Freight Corridor (DFC) for Murbe Port, Maharashtra

• 🚛 Commenced commercial operations at Arakkonam GCT, expanding the logistics business

• 💰 Successfully completed ₹7,503 crore QIP, strengthening growth capital, meeting MPS norms and attracting marquee investors

Vedanta Oil & Gas | Business Update

Legal Update
- ONGC seeks enforcement of $307 million arbitral award.
- Petition filed in Delhi High Court.
- Next hearing on 11 September 2026.

Company's Position
- Amount already provided for in books.
- No interim adverse order passed.
- Evaluating legal remedies.

Key Takeaway
- The arbitration enforcement petition represents an ongoing legal matter for Vedanta Oil & Gas, with no immediate operational impact as the company has already provided for the amount in its financial statements.

How inflection reflects in financial return ratios of a business?

Following is the example of KVB:

1. ROE expanded from 16% to 18% almost.
2. ROA went from 1.63% to a run rate of 2.11%
3. Net interest margins have improved non stop over last 5 Qs.
4. Cost to income has fallen thereby reflecting operating leverage in the business.

These inflection lasts for 2-3 years before new opex has to be incurred by a business to grow further.

Just a small example on how infections occur.

Keep tracking improvement in return ratios over every Quarterly to semi annual and annual results

Financial Highlights
- Cargo volumes 31 MT (+6% YoY).

- Arakkonam GCT commercialised.

Balance Sheet
- Strong capital position.
- Expansion funded comfortably.
- Leverage expected to remain healthy.
- Investment-grade credit profile.

Impact
- Positive.
- Capacity expansion on track.
- Logistics business scaling rapidly.
- Long-term earnings visibility improving.

Key Takeaway
- JSW Infrastructure continues to execute an aggressive expansion strategy across ports and logistics. With a strong balance sheet, sizeable growth capex, improving logistics contribution and a target to more than double cargo handling capacity by FY30, the company remains well positioned for sustained long-term growth.

growth as well as pass through of higher pricing to mitigate raw material cost inflation. EBIT increased
during both periods, benefiting from volume leverage, an improved product mix, and operational
efficiency gains, despite the impact of certain non-recurring charges and geo-political challenges.
Aligned with its global strategic priorities, the company continued to emphasize profitable growth,
disciplined capital allocation, and a strong culture of accountability.
As a part of Huhtamaki Strategy 2030, our ambition is to be the first choice in sustainable packaging
solutions.

TCI: ₹81.96 CRORE INCOME TAX DEMAND REDUCED TO NIL ✅💰

• ⚖️ Transport Corporation of India (TCI) said the Income Tax Department has rectified computational errors in its assessment for AY 2024-25, following the company's rectification application. 

• 💰 The earlier income tax demand of ₹81.96 crore has been fully extinguished, with the outstanding demand now reduced to Nil after allowing appropriate TDS/TCS credits. 

• 📝 The Rectification Order under Section 154 of the Income Tax Act has been passed by the Jurisdictional Assessing Officer and given effect by the Centralized Processing Centre (CPC). 

• 📊 The company stated there is no outstanding tax demand for AY 2024-25 and no adverse financial, operational or other impact arising from the earlier assessment order. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The elimination of the ₹81.96 crore tax demand removes a major overhang and strengthens TCI's financial position.)

COFORGE: NAMED 'EXCEPTIONAL PERFORMER' IN UK & IRELAND IT SOURCING STUDY 🏆☁️

• 🏅 Coforge has been recognized as an 'Exceptional Performer' in Application Services, Cloud & Infrastructure Services, and General Satisfaction in Whitelane Research's 2026 UK & Ireland IT Sourcing Study. It was also named a Strong Performer in Security Services. 

• 📊 In Application Services, Coforge ranked 2nd with a customer satisfaction score of 83%, outperforming the market average of 76%. 

• ☁️ In Cloud & Infrastructure Services, the company scored 83% versus the 75% market average, recording the largest year-on-year improvement in the category with a 10 percentage point increase. 

• 🌍 The recognition is based on feedback from nearly 400 participants and 1,000+ IT sourcing relationships across the UK & Ireland, marking the fourth consecutive year that Coforge has been acknowledged in the study. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The recognition strengthens Coforge's brand credibility, enhances its competitive positioning in Europe and supports future client wins in digital and cloud services.)

• 🤝 Velox Shipping & Logistics has completed the acquisition of a 98% stake in International Logistics Associates LLC (ILA), USA, after fulfilling all transaction conditions. 

• 💰 The company has paid the entire consideration of USD 447,860 (around US$0.45 million) for the acquisition, with the transaction completed on July 20, 2026.

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition strengthens Velox's international footprint, expands its logistics capabilities in the US market and supports long-term growth.)

ANTONY WASTE: Q1 TONNAGE UP 5.4%, REFINANCES WTE LOAN AT LOWER RATE ♻️📈

• 🚛 Antony Waste managed ~1.40 million tonnes of waste in Q1 FY27, up ~5.4% YoY, while Collection & Transportation volumes grew ~5.1% and Processing volumes increased ~5.5%. Core revenue rose ~7% YoY. 

• 💰 The company refinanced the term loan of its Pimpri Chinchwad Waste-to-Energy (WtE) facility, reducing the interest rate from 10.25% to 8.25%, lowering borrowing costs by 200 bps and improving free cash flow. 

• ⚠️ Following the July 8, 2026 force majeure incident at the PCMC WtE facility, the company confirmed 9 employee fatalities, announced ₹25 lakh financial assistance per deceased employee, employment support for next of kin, and education support for minor children where required. 

• ♻️ The MRF and composting plant have resumed operations, while the company remains committed to the phased restart of the remaining WtE facility and will provide quarterly updates on recovery efforts. 

🟡 Impact: Mixed (Operational performance and lower financing costs are positive, but the tragic accident and phased restart of the WtE facility remain key near-term concerns.)

• 🎥 Connplex Cinemas expanded its network to 44 operational cinemas (+63% YoY), 122 screens (+72% YoY), 10,421 seats (+88% YoY), with presence across 32 cities in 10 states. 

• 👥 Admissions increased 36% YoY to 7.04 lakh, while the company signed 43 new screens during Q1 FY27, supporting future expansion. 

• 💰 Monetisation improved with Average Ticket Price (ATP) rising 7% YoY to ₹253 and Spend Per Head (SPH) increasing 6% YoY to ₹105, reflecting stronger pricing and higher F&B spending. 

• 🚀 The company remains focused on accelerating screen additions in underpenetrated markets and expects the 43 signed screens to support sustained long-term growth. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong network expansion, higher footfalls and improving monetisation metrics strengthen Connplex's long-term growth outlook.)

VIVIANA POWER TECH: COMMISSIONS 400 KV TRANSMISSION LINE FOR 323.4 MW WIND PARK ⚡🌬️

• ⚡ Viviana Power Tech has successfully commissioned a 400 kV Double-Circuit LILO transmission line for a 323.4 MW wind park in Kachchh, Gujarat, with the line being energised on July 10, 2026. 

• 🏗️ The project involved executing three high-voltage crossings, including a 765 kV under-crossing, ±500 kV HVDC under-crossing and a 400 kV overhead crossing, along with live-line stringing without a grid shutdown. 

• 📊 As of 21 July 2026, the company has a confirmed order book of ~₹1,340 crore (including BESS), bids under evaluation worth ~₹936 crore, and identified tenders exceeding ₹2,500 crore. 

• 🌱 The commissioned transmission line enables evacuation of renewable power from the wind park to the high-voltage grid, strengthening India's clean energy transmission infrastructure. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The successful commissioning showcases Viviana's execution capabilities, strengthens its credentials in EHV transmission projects and enhances long-term growth visibility backed by a strong order pipeline.)

reports.text:
India paid an additional ₹2.02 trillion for crude oil imports in the AMJ 2026 quarter.

This was higher by 77%, despite volume dropping by 4.5%.

Axis has come up with a Buy report on 
Steel Strip Wheels (SSWL) with a target price of 305.

However, 2 things to note - 
1> Their estimates are petty conservative, management guidance is actually higher with a positive bias. So, EPS could very well be 10-15% higher. 

2> They have valued the company at 14 PE.
I am not sure why. If the company cracks the export market and is able to grow at 20%+ with margin upside, I am not sure if it will trade at such low valuations.

SO, the bigger opportunity here is the valuation rerating.
If market decides that it deserves a 25-30 PE which is still lower than most auto ancillary stocks, then this can be a huge winner. Lets see how this goes.

reports.text:
Goldman Sachs initiates coverage on Jindal Stainless 
BUY rating and TP at ₹1,000 

Key reasons behind the bullish stance:
✅ Best placed to benefit from rising domestic stainless steel consumption
✅ Higher share of value-added products to support profitability
✅ Strong balance sheet provides headroom to nearly double capacity from the current 4.2 MTPA over the medium term
✅ Attractive valuations relative to expected RoE

A side note: Amit Dixit the author of the report has been constructive on Jindal Stainless since much lower price levels during his time at previous brokerage houses.

**Paytm | CLSA View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- EBITDA reported at **₹200 crore**.
- EBITDA marginally beat estimates.
- Payments GMV growth accelerated to **31% YoY**.
- Financial services revenue growth strengthened.

**Growth Drivers**
- Merchant payments gaining momentum.
- Financial services scaling well.
- Stable contribution margin at **55%**.
- Lower fixed costs boosted profitability.

**Key Monitorables**
- Payment take rate declined sequentially.
- Lower doubtful debt provisions aided EBITDA.
- Wallet license application submitted.
- Higher operating expenses expected ahead.

**Brokerage View**
- **CLSA:** **Outperform**.
- Target Price **₹1,050**.
- FY27–FY29 EBITDA estimates cut **2–3%**.
- Limited upside after recent stock rally.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA acknowledges Paytm's strong operational momentum in payments and financial services, but believes much of the optimism around a possible UPI MDR return is already reflected in the stock price. While profitability continues to improve, higher operating expenses could moderate earnings growth.**

**Paytm | Citi View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Revenue grew **28% YoY**.
- EBITDA reached **₹200 crore**.
- EBITDA beat estimates by **16%**.
- Contribution profit at **55%** of revenue.

**Growth Drivers**
- Higher merchant loan distribution.
- Lower cloud infrastructure costs.
- Merchant ecosystem expanding.
- Operating leverage improving.

**Key Monitorables**
- Payment margins impacted by device rentals.
- Potential UPI MDR implementation.
- MDR could improve payment margins.
- Earnings estimates revised upward.

**Brokerage View**
- **Citi:** **Buy**.
- Target Price raised to **₹1,560**.
- Estimates increased after Q1.
- FY28 EBITDA could rise **8–10%** if UPI MDR is introduced.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains bullish on Paytm, supported by strong operating performance, improving cost efficiency and robust merchant lending growth. A potential introduction of UPI MDR on large merchants could provide an additional boost to payment margins and future earnings.**

**Paytm | Goldman Sachs View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Strong quarterly performance.
- Revenue growth accelerated.
- EBITDA beat estimates by **20%**.
- Bonus issue plans deferred.

**Growth Drivers**
- Offline payment market share gains.
- Online merchant additions accelerated.
- Merchant loan distribution remained strong.
- Consumer postpaid business expanding.

**Business Outlook**
- Payment ecosystem gaining momentum.
- Merchant acquisition continues.
- Consumer lending supports growth.
- Profitability expected to improve sharply.

**Brokerage View**
- **Goldman Sachs:** **Buy**.
- Target Price **₹1,500**.
- FY27 revenue growth estimated at **27% YoY**.
- FY27 EBITDA expected to more than double.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Goldman Sachs remains positive on Paytm, driven by accelerating payments growth, sustained merchant lending traction and expanding consumer financial products. The brokerage expects strong revenue momentum and a sharp improvement in profitability through FY27.**
Emcure IC

**UltraTech Cement | Jefferies View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Strong execution continued.
- EBITDA grew **14% YoY**.
- Double-digit volume growth.
- Better pricing supported earnings.

**Growth Drivers**
- Healthy volume momentum.
- Stable pricing outlook.
- Strong operational execution.
- Capacity expansion progressing.

**Management Outlook**
- Pricing expected stable through monsoon.
- Peak industry costs anticipated.
- Double-digit volume growth expected in FY27.
- EBITDA/tonne target **₹1,400** by **Q4FY28**.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** **Buy**.
- Target Price **₹14,065**.
- Positive on earnings trajectory.
- Confident on margin expansion.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains bullish on UltraTech Cement, citing superior execution, sustained double-digit volume growth, stable pricing and continued margin improvement. The brokerage expects the company to achieve ₹1,400 EBITDA per tonne by Q4FY28, supported by strong operational discipline and industry-leading scale.**

**UltraTech Cement | JPMorgan View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- PAT reported at **₹2,600 crore**.
- EBITDA/tonne at **₹1,214**.
- Volumes exceeded expectations.
- Quarter delivered a slight beat.

**Growth Drivers**
- Higher domestic volumes.
- Scale advantage remains strong.
- ICEM profitability improving.
- Market leadership intact.

**Key Monitorables**
- QoQ costs increased.
- Price hikes offset by costs.
- Q2 may remain challenging.
- Monsoon could impact demand.

**Management Outlook**
- ICEM EBITDA/tonne target **₹1,000**.
- Scale to support resilience.
- Better positioned than peers.
- Long-term outlook remains positive.

**Brokerage View**
- **JPMorgan:** **Overweight**.
- Target Price **₹13,300**.
- Positive on execution.
- Scale remains competitive advantage.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan remains constructive on UltraTech Cement, citing stronger-than-expected volume growth, improving ICEM profitability and unmatched scale, which should help the company outperform peers despite near-term cost pressures and seasonal demand challenges.**

**UltraTech Cement | Citi View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Consolidated EBITDA grew **14% YoY**.
- India volumes increased **13% YoY**.
- Realizations improved **1% YoY**.
- India EBITDA/tonne at **₹1,208**.

**Growth Drivers**
- Strong domestic volume growth.
- Realizations rose **~4% QoQ**.
- Cost management remained effective.
- ICL profitability improved significantly.

**Management Outlook**
- EBITDA/tonne target **₹1,400** by **Q4FY28E**.
- Q2 costs to rise **₹130–140/tonne**.
- Industry expects stable pricing.
- India expansion progressing as planned.

**Capital Allocation**
- Growth investments remain priority.
- Dividend policy unchanged.
- Net Debt/EBITDA expected below **1x**.
- Next expansion phase to be announced.

**Brokerage View**
- **Citi:** **Buy**.
- Target Price **₹14,750**.
- Positive on growth and cost control.
- Attractive valuation versus historical peak.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains bullish on UltraTech Cement, driven by strong volume growth, disciplined cost management, improving profitability and a robust expansion pipeline. Healthy balance sheet strength and continued capacity additions support sustained long-term earnings growth.**

**UltraTech Cement | CLSA View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Strong quarter across metrics.
- Volume grew **13% YoY**.
- EBITDA per tonne **₹1,183**.
- Performance beat industry trends.

**Growth Drivers**
- Better-than-expected cost control.
- Robust execution continued.
- Capacity expansion on track.
- Margin accretion expected.

**Management Outlook**
- Q2 cost increase guided at **₹130–140/tonne**.
- Lower than normal seasonal inflation.
- Earnings outlook remains constructive.
- Strong free cash flow expected.

**Brokerage View**
- **CLSA:** **High Conviction Outperform**.
- Target Price **₹14,000**.
- Mid-teen earnings CAGR expected over **FY26–30**.
- Improving ROCE supports valuation.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA remains highly positive on UltraTech Cement, citing strong execution, superior cost control, ongoing capacity expansion and improving margins, which are expected to drive mid-teen earnings growth, higher ROCE and robust free cash flow over the medium term.**

**Oberoi Realty | HSBC View**

**Investment Thesis**
- Premium residential developer.
- Strong launch pipeline ahead.
- Healthy demand environment.
- Quality portfolio remains key strength.

**Growth Drivers**
- Gurugram launch boosts outlook.
- New launches set to accelerate.
- Premium housing demand remains strong.
- Sales momentum expected to improve.

**Business Outlook**
- Q1 pre-sales remained soft.
- Q2 launch addresses concerns.
- Execution pipeline remains robust.
- Residential momentum likely sustained.

**Brokerage View**
- **HSBC:** **Buy**.
- Target Price **₹2,310**.
- Positive on launch pipeline.
- Confident on medium-term growth.

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC remains bullish on Oberoi Realty, believing the strong Gurugram launch and an accelerating pipeline of premium residential projects will drive sustained pre-sales growth and reinforce the company's leadership in the luxury real estate segment.**

**SBI Funds Management | Emkay View**

**Brokerage View**
- **Emkay:** **Buy (Initiation)**.
- Target Price **₹750**.
- Positive on brand strength.
- Favourable structural industry outlook.

**Investment Thesis**
- Leading domestic asset manager.
- Strong brand and distribution network.
- Large under-penetrated market opportunity.
- Well-positioned for long-term growth.

**Growth Drivers**
- Healthy AUM growth expected.
- Revenue and profitability to improve.
- Equity and alternate assets gaining share.
- Higher-yield asset mix supports margins.

**Financial Outlook**
- Operating leverage to strengthen earnings.
- Scale benefits improving profitability.
- EBITDA CAGR of **~17%** expected over **FY26–29**.
- Long-term growth visibility remains strong.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Emkay has initiated coverage on SBI Funds Management with a Buy rating, highlighting its dominant brand, extensive distribution network and strong structural tailwinds from rising financialisation. A shift toward higher-yielding asset classes and operating leverage are expected to drive sustained earnings growth.**
